mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or the signal fails to predict direction with > 50% accuracy over 2015-2017 data.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth expectations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative or zero 60-day forward return in > 60% of triggered cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity utilization, which boosts corporate profit margins and equity valuations, but only during periods of stable inflation where input costs do not erode margins.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO trends coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 2.5% annually, or the mechanism shows zero predictive power outside the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth revisions upward reflect stronger aggregate economic activity, which immediately improves market sentiment and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Upward GDP revisions fail to produce positive 5-day forward returns in > 50% of cases, or the signal out-of-sample Sharpe ratio remains below 0.5 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation surpasses a 3.0% annualized threshold, markets anticipate aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in > 65% of periods where CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3.0%, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions significantly (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal yields negative 20-day returns in > 55% of observations, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals that higher borrowing costs are successfully suppressing economic activity, leading to immediate profit downgrades and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO trends during tightening cycles produce positive 5-day SPX returns in > 60% of cases, or the regime-specific predictive accuracy drops below 45% in walk-forward testing.
